74015.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Blockchain Association, former security officials urge Senate to pass Clarity Act
74015.body:
Cardano's Charles Hoskinson warns of 'wave of failures' after TapTools wind-down | Mastercard expands stablecoin settlement options with USDC, PYUSD and RLUSD | Kraken to offer tokenized US IPO access for global retail investors via xStocks | Binance winds down centralized NFT service, gives users one month to withdraw assets |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin* slipped below $66,000 this morning as analysts pointed ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403454/bitcoin-dips-below-66000?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) to heavy ETF outflows, Middle East tensions, and lingering fallout from *Strategy's* *BTC* sale.

In today's newsletter, former national security and law enforcement officials urge the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , *Cardano's Charles Hoskinson* warns of a "wave of failures," *Mastercard* expands its stablecoin settlement options, and more.

Meanwhile, *Sen. Lummis* said ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403539/sen-lummis-jpmorgan-ceo-jamie-dimon-hasnt-read-clarity-act-remarks-distasteful?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *JPMorgan* CEO *Jamie Dimon* hasn't read the *Clarity Act* , calling his recent remarks about *Coinbase* CEO *Brian Armstrong* "distasteful."

Plus, *Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent* backed ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403563/bessent-backs-summer-push-clarity-act-bitcoin-reserve-moving-deliberate-speed?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a summer push for passage of the *Clarity Act* , adding that the department is moving at a "deliberate speed" to establish a strategic *bitcoin* reserve.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🏛️ Blockchain Association urges Senate to pass Clarity Act with letter from 160 former security officials*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/403450/blockchain-association-letter-senate?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *Blockchain Association* urged the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , backing its push with a letter signed by 160 former national security and law enforcement officials.

* The former officials argued the bill would strengthen law enforcement capabilities and improve ef

Гроші рухаються додому
**********************************

Money moves homes 
( http://clicks.paysend.com/f/a/9zbc3npulb796pK7cSouDQ~~/AAAHahA~/p6NUUz81tUvlan0TkyQtTNczjJbHjkfnJFD7lH4Fd_s3ENciD2b8ZyirFEU6JYxbjUyF0-C-hVdGWvqKIIoyl1UYqHEKzhh_CMkGmztmhKgYhLq6ZDqBVn6RnEIHV6mIXThFx0WInq22isP7FMNJmw~~ )

*****************************************************************

Привіт
Oleh,

*****************************************************************

Відправлення за відправленням, рік за роком - ви були там. Для людей, які на вас розраховують.

Padala. 家. Pix. Parivar. Рідні. Bendición. Hashar. Naija. Parce. ოჯახი. Otbasy. Bhalobasha. Mi gente. Близкие. Gurbet. Ашар. Acasă. Меҳр. Rodzina. Pavula. Ailə. Dor. Տուն.

І багато іншого. Різні слова для однієї дії.

А під ним - інфраструктура, яку ми побудували, щоб кожен літак міг приземлитися. Майже десять років. Надійно. З упевненістю, яка вам потрібна.

Дякуємо за кожну посилку додому. Не зупиняйся. Гроші рухаються.

Відправляємо додому 
( http://clicks.paysend.com/f/a/2HjbO_OvMTtR00zxJy60qg~~/AAAHahA~/p6NUUz81tUvlan0TkyQtTNczjJbHjkfnJFD7lH4Fd_vG0_Xy6yGZ3arE7QJpvx8dCI3cwhsib10qaJUDrJKnWrSuAzSI1u3-oYEi4K4-CDxb-IFq5anXxg

74013.subject: Ethereum Breaks. Saylor Blinks.
74013.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**June 3, 2026 · ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dddf2f20-4042-4b28-b237-fd6d7f5b23aa/image.png?t=1780466800)
Caption: Market Snapshot  · As of June 3_  |  _BTC Price _ |  _YTD Return _ |  _Fear & Greed _ |  _ETF Flows (Weekly) _ _

## 📡** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

This week, crypto and the rest of the market filed for divorce. The S&P 500 hit its 19th all-time high of 2026, the Dow crossed 51,000, and Bitcoin slid to $67,000.

Spot Bitcoin ETFs hit a record 10-day outflow streak. Ether ETFs ran 14 and counting. Ether broke $2,000. And a U.S. court order forced Circle to freeze $12.6M of stablecoins inside an Ethereum privacy protocol.

_The AI trade is having a great year. Crypto is having a week._

_**Let's get into it…**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8132e416-70db-495d-8fec-7c68d66b46d5/image.png?t=1778654852)
Caption: 

----------
# 🐋 THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. ****Ethereum's Worst Week of 2026**

Ether broke below $2,000 on May 28 for the first time since late March, capping a brutal stretch that has now wiped 32% off ETH's year-to-date gains. Spot Ether ETFs extended their losing streak past 14 sessions, draining hundreds of millions, while the ETH/BTC ratio kept sliding.

The Ethereum Foundation also lost two more high-profile researchers, Carl Beekhuizen and Julian Ma, deepening the talent-flight narrative. The one bright spot: BitMine, Tom Lee's corporate buyer, kept hoovering up ETH, lifting its stash above $11 billion. Conviction is still there, just not in the price.

_**Meanwhile, Bitcoin saw its own corporate headline:**_ Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) sold 32 BTC (around $2.5M) for the first time since 2022, as mentioned in a[ June 1 filing](https://assets.contentstack.io/v3/assets/bltf8d808d9b8cebd37/blt01aedf36c9f1b5b3/6a1cdb95487e7818fe49dd85/form-8-k_06-01-2026.pdf), to fund preferred stock dividends, a symbolic break from the "never sell" playbook.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b6312343-3c6f-4fec-bf96-c18493c14410/image.png?t=1780466894)
Caption: _Chart: Bitcoin price over the past 7 days_

### **2. ****Circle Drops the Freeze Button on $12.6M**

On May 30, USDC issuer Circle blacklisted the smart contract behind Zama's confidential USDC (cUSDC) wrapper, freezing roughly $12.6 million inside. The trigger was a federal court order tied to a class-action against Overnight Finance, whose founder allegedly moved $15.77M from the treasury and parked $12.4M in Zama's pool on May 11.

_**The catch? **_Zama and most of its users had nothing to do with the alleged fraud. Innocent cUSDC holders w

74012.subject: Want to know the future? Don’t trust the stockmarket
74012.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWprNcd9pzFdKugAM__RdmyDiaaE1PRp4rUz6yWqmThJdD2ADU-p_lWWRkSVQLvXvZ-fLJh4Rgz_Jn43C8RMH7wk3ZXndZcxY8e0b_J 

June 3rd 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWqsYUJGatb-mqBfMGlcsEXcX5hg4ZIs4L-CRTodiZbS87QQTgGg_Ru2Z5Sn-SvkHfnaPn9iVN_FsARTwdU2_a0ejQRyVjEqeTOy8Tg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWr5av7uHLtMHrW-ZUKmALGDFptk9tDA8IS_MmPYrZxlrRO3nQokmEth1gZ0QdXO678nAS1nemCHZUxq5uOqowzzSMcwtPhjo5RLxKC 



 
 

Lane Greene

Senior digital editor



Many financial economists believe in the efficient-markets hypothesis: that stockmarket investors accurately absorb new information and reprice shares to reflect companies’ underlying profitability. But 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWsviTyx78Hy6tw_jVV573ycEInWfKSVy6mdLZAG8JyzvIQMDuGYDFBQe77s1T2soWlY5uFn1ILfQp-6Kh9gayAUZXBTIaYDhE 
Buttonwood takes a critical view , showing why markets can be much more volatile (often on the upside) than efficient-market reasoning can explain.

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-3-oleh-bezuhlyi-l4trf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BJmdavfjuTw6xeAMAoyb%2BBQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=03GT-Qq_gGPIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpy9jg9j~f-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpy9jg9j-f
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Deep liquidations and record ETF outflows have triggered a sharp transition to extreme fear. While payment giants prepare massive stablecoin infrastructure, short-term panic dominates as MicroStrategy and Mt. Gox signal potential supply pressure. 📉Bitcoin Crashes Below $66K as $1.8B Liquidations Rock Market Bitcoin plummeted to an intraday low of $65,710 following a record 11-day streak of ETF outflows totaling $3.5 billion. The volatility triggered the largest leverage flush since February, wiping out $1.35 billion in long positions and dragging the total market cap down to $2.3 trillion. Read full story 🏛️MicroStrategy Records First Bitcoin Sale Since 2022 to Fund Dividends Michael Saylor’s MicroStrategy (now Strategy) disclosed the sale of 32 BTC to fund distributions for its preferred stock (STRC). Although the sale represents a fraction of its 843,706 BTC treasury, the symbolic departure from its "never-sell" strategy sparked institutional concern, sending shares down 9%. See details 🚨Mt. Gox Moves $739M in Bitcoin Spooking Creditor Repayment Fears The defunct exchange Mt. Gox transferred 10,422 BTC to a new, unlabeled wallet in its first major movement in over six months. The transfer has heightened market anxiety regarding potential selling pressure ahead of the final October 2026 creditor repayment deadline. Read more 💰Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard Near Launch of Joint Stablecoin Platform Payment giants Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard are reportedly collaborating on a unified stablecoin infrastructure to modernize global settlement. The platform aims to provide internet-native financial primitives for 24/7 cross-border transactions, with Coinbase reportedly evaluating participation in the initiative. See full report ⚠️Winklevoss Twins Move $67M BTC to Gemini Sparking Sell-Off Rumors Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss transferred 1,000 BTC from cold storage to a Gemini-linked hot wallet as Bitcoin hit multi-month lows. While the intent remains unconfirmed, on-chain analysts at Arkham note that such transfers to exchange wallets historically precede institutional distribution. Track on-chain data 🏛️Warren and Sanders Oppose Proposal for Crypto in 401(k) Plans Senators Elizabeth Warren and Bernie Sanders have urged the Department of Labor to withdraw a proposal allowing crypto in retirement accounts. Lawma

74006.subject: The Career Skills AI Can’t Replace, According To AMD CEO Lisa Su
74006.body:
Forbes Edge

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/career-skills-ai-cant-replace-according-amd-ceo-lisa-su-lcx5c?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BsRlN%2BaR9QJqtDAlcmlW1wQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3TMOcwtf39Psg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpy4zkr8~dk-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpy4zkr8-dk
  
At a time when many professionals are worried that artificial intelligence could short circuit their careers, Advanced Micro Devices CEO Lisa Su has offered a bigger-picture perspective about the critical role humans will continue to play despite major technological advancements.  In a recent commencement speech at her alma mater MIT, Su—who came in at No. 10 on Forbes’ 2025 list of the World’s Most Powerful Women—told graduates: “For everything that AI can do, AI can’t decide which problems are worth solving. It can’t make the hard judgments when the data is not there. It can’t take responsibility for the outcomes. These are actually our responsibilities and they matter now more than ever.” The tech CEO recounted other technology shifts of the past, including the birth of the internet, which changed how we communicate; mobile computing, which changed how we live; and cloud computing, which changed how we work. But unlike those previous developments, Su said that AI “has the potential to accelerate discovery in every field and help us solve problems that we’ve never been able to solve before.”  Nevertheless, she added, “Technology itself does not decide what the future looks like—the best people do.”  Read here to learn more about Su’s perspective on AI and why she says we don’t need more people who simply know how to use the tool—we need people who know how to apply it to solve big problems.  — Courtney Connley-Hampton, Careers Staff Writer WORK SMARTER  Practical insights and advice from Forbes staff and contributors to help you succeed in your job, accelerate your career and lead smarter. Not hearing back after applying to a job? Learn three reasons this happens—and key steps to fix this frustrating part of the job search—with strategies from career expert Colleen Batchelder. If you feel stuck in a job due to money, you can still make moves toward a pivot. Discover five solutions to the cash trap in a job you no longer love from career coach Caroline Ceniza-Levine. Are layoffs coming to your company? Here are 10 things a CEO may say to signal potential cuts—including “aligning resources to our highest priorities”—from career expert Mark Murphy. A coworker who starts a debate in every discussion can erode psychological safety on a team. Leaders should address this behavior to ensure ideas are heard, professor Benjamin Laker says. Deep Dive: Why Gen Z
